title: Merge Bases, Topology, and Integration Strategies · Graph Lens: Gita Merge Result Defense
summary: Two release lines contain criss-cross merges, a reverted integration, rename-heavy work, and policy requiring explainable ancestry; the integrator must choose and defend topology. Planned artifact: Add and verify the gita merge result defense increment in merge topology and strategy decision record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Verify merge parents, combined diff, resulting tree, tests, release invariants, signatures, and rollback path before publication.
Recognize when octopus, subtree, or unrelated-history integration is appropriate and when staged pairwise review is safer.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Predict Gita Merge Result Defense
steps.instruction:
theory case — Case G39 is a theory in a disposable repository. criss-cross integration yields more than one best common ancestor. The learner must predict all best bases are listed so a synthetic merge base and strategy risk can be reviewed, run git merge-base --all release topic, explain two incomparable bases make a single-base mental model unsafe for this integration, test this changed condition: linearize one side branch and observe the candidate set collapse to one merge base, and preserve this recovery boundary: preserve both tips under refs/integration/before-merge before testing a strategy.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Gita Merge Result Defense contract for the supplied data.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for verify merge parents, combined diff, resulting tree, tests, release invariants, signatures, and rollback path before publication.

content.text: Verify merge parents, combined diff, resulting tree, tests, release invariants, signatures, and rollback path before publication. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Locate repository and operation state, compare working tree, index, refs, and graph, preserve a recovery reference, preview the smallest change, then verify both tree behavior and history topology.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Case G39 is a worked example in a disposable repository. criss-cross integration yields more than one best common ancestor. The learner must predict all best bases are listed so a synthetic merge base and strategy risk can be reviewed, run git merge-base --all release topic, explain two incomparable bases make a single-base mental model unsafe for this integration, test this changed condition: linearize one side branch and observe the candidate set collapse to one merge base, and preserve this recovery boundary: preserve both tips under refs/integration/before-merge before testing a strategy. A defensible solution applies Gita Merge Result Defense, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to merge topology and strategy decision record.

content.code:
# Advanced Git 2.55 deterministic scenario 2
# before-ref: main=a11ce00 candidate=b00b1e5
# hypothesis: criss-cross integration yields more than one best common ancestor
git merge-base --all release topic
# expected: all best bases are listed so a synthetic merge base and strategy risk can be reviewed
# observation: two incomparable bases make a single-base mental model unsafe for this integration
# changed-case: linearize one side branch and observe the candidate set collapse to one merge base
# after-ref: main=a11ce00 candidate=b00b1e5
# tree-proof: compare the exact affected tree and paths before judging success
# behavior-proof: retain a changed-case test or invariant that can falsify the Git-only conclusion
# recovery: preserve both tips under refs/integration/before-merge before testing a strategy
# transfer-limit: validate resulting behavior in an authorized disposable clone because graph evidence is not semantic proof
content.caption: Runnable repository-state trace example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: A conflict-free merge proves both sides remain behaviorally compatible. This fails the Gita Merge Result Defense contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

stimulus.title: Gita Merge Result Defense failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed

options.text: Keep the artifact unchanged and rewrite the explanation so the current output appears intentional.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.

steps.title: Build Gita Merge Result Defense evidence
steps.instruction: Append an Advanced Git evidence block headed "# Evidence: gita-merge-result-defense-amznjf" with exact before and after refs; a falsifiable prediction; the bounded simulator command; expected, observed, tree, behavior, changed-case, and recovery evidence; and one honest real-repository transfer limit.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Gita Merge Result Defense into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: gita-merge-result-defense-amznjf
# scenario-key: trace-amznjf probe-fjnzma boundary-znjfam owner-amznjfx fault-amznjfz
# before-ref: main=a11ceam candidate=b00b1jf
# hypothesis: criss-cross integration yields more than one best common ancestor
git merge-base --all release topic
# expected: all best bases are listed so a synthetic merge base and strategy risk can be reviewed
# observation: two incomparable bases make a single-base mental model unsafe for this integration
# changed-case: linearize one side branch and observe the candidate set collapse to one merge base
# after-ref: main=a11ceam candidate=c0ffeef
# tree-proof: the affected tree and path set match the predicted repository transition
# behavior-proof: a changed input preserves the named product or release invariant
# recovery: preserve both tips under refs/integration/before-merge before testing a strategy
# transfer-limit: validate resulting behavior in an authorized disposable clone because graph evidence is not semantic proof
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence defense
